memory_recovered: The project queue is an event stream over a dated canonical baseline. A safe materializer must validate team ownership from item prefixes, order updates deterministically, retain provenance, preserve owner conflicts instead of overwriting them, and emit only a candidate compaction until a reviewed canonical merge occurs.

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json predates later append-only queue updates.
M-011 explicitly proposed a deterministic queue-state materializer.
The specialist registry already contains the required continuity roles.
inferred: A deterministic candidate compaction reduces false negatives and cross-team clobbering risk.
Owner-prefix validation is a practical enforcement of team-owned queue records.
proposed: Enumerate all queue-update files in an authenticated checkout and feed them to the materializer.
Add reference verification for output commits and evidence paths before canonical compaction.
superseded: Treating absence from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as proof that a later item never existed.
Silently choosing one side of an owner conflict.
unresolved: Complete enumeration of all queue-update files was not available through the connector search surface.
Referenced evidence and commit existence are not yet verified by the materializer.
No canonical compaction has been produced or published.

verification.scope: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source later committed to the repository

privacy_boundary: Included only public repository paths, blob and commit identifiers, abstract project semantics, and test results. Excluded raw private chat, health and pet-health material, credentials, personal identifiers, and unreviewed uploads.
limits: The connector did not provide a complete directory listing of every queue update.
The materializer does not prove referenced commits, evidence, deployments, peer runs, or human outcomes.
The canonical queue was not overwritten.
